title: NOC 72014 – Contractors and supervisors, other construction trades, installers, repairers and servicers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Contractors and Supervisors (NOC 72014) serve as the essential "multi-trade" leaders across Canada’s residential and commercial landscape. This unit group is uniquely diverse, overseeing specialized trades that complete the building envelope and interior—from bricklayers and roofers to painters, drywallers, and specialized repairers of everything from bicycles to vending machines. As Canada pushes for rapid housing deployment and energy-efficient building retrofits, these supervisors are the tactical managers ensuring that specialized sub-trades work in harmony, safely, and to the high-performance standards required by 2026 building codes.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Usually requires a college diploma, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

common_job_titles: Repair and Service Establishment Manager (Bicycles, Cameras, Vending Machines)
duties: Trade Supervision: Coordinating and scheduling the activities of bricklayers, roofers, painters, glaziers, and other specialized installers or repairers.
Technical Quality Control: Establishing methods to meet work schedules and coordinating activities with other subcontractors to ensure seamless project delivery.
Resource Logistics: Requisitioning materials and supplies and resolving technical work problems to improve productivity and finish quality.
Training & Safety: Mentoring apprentices and ensuring that all workers follow 2026 Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) standards for heights, chemicals, and heavy materials.
Reporting & HR: Preparing work progress reports and recommending personnel actions such as hirings, promotions, or specialized training.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Experience: Several years of experience as a qualified Journeyperson in the specific trade being supervised are mandatory.
Certification: Journeyperson trade certification in a relevant trade (e.g., Bricklayer, Roofer, Painter) is often required.
Specialized Licensing: Certain roles (e.g., Petroleum Mechanics or Refueling Station Installers) require specific provincial certification (e.g., TSSA in Ontario).
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, with over 19,800 job openings projected nationally through 2031, primarily driven by the need to replace retiring journey-level leaders:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for this NOC under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. Minimum scores varied by region (e.g., 53 in Southwestern Ontario and 54 in Northern Ontario), highlighting a critical need for supervisors outside the GTA.
Alberta: Strong Demand. Alberta offers the highest earning potential for this NOC, with top-tier wages reaching $75.86/hr and a median annual salary of approximately $85,546.
British Columbia: High Opportunity. Specifically in the "Mainland/Southwest" region, BC projects nearly 4,000 openings in related construction trades through 2035, with median wages at $37.50/hr.
Atlantic Canada: Consistent Demand. Provinces like Nova Scotia and New Brunswick are prioritizing this NOC for Regional Economic Development draws to support growing urban centers.
Why Work in Canada as a Multi-Trade Supervisor?
Lead the Prefab & Retrofit Boom: Gain expertise in 2026-grade technologies, including Exterior Insulation Finishing Systems (EIFS) and the installation of high-efficiency prefabricated components.
Top-Tier Trades Compensation: As a TEER 2 professional, your median hourly wage in Canada is $37.50/hr, with high-earners in provinces like Alberta reaching upwards of $58.11/hr.
Entrepreneurial Versatility: This NOC has one of the highest rates of self-employment (nearly 40% in some regions), allowing you to manage your own specialty contracting firm.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC remains a consistent target for Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) "In-Demand" and "Skilled Trades" draws.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): In March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72014 under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a high-success route for those with a valid job offer in Ontario's regional industrial sectors.

2026 TR to PR Pathway: A newly confirmed federal initiative for 2026–2027 targeting 33,000 temporary workers. Multi-trade supervisors are classified as "High-Priority Infrastructure and Housing Workers."
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Trades): As a TEER 2 technical trade, it is eligible for category-based draws for skilled trades, which typically have lower CRS requirements than general draws.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for supervisors with a job offer from a BC-based specialty contracting firm, particularly in the Mainland/Southwest region.
